503.description: The Health Check is not successful

get.summary: List active documents for one active person record

post.summary: Create a document for one active person record

get.summary: Get one active document for one active person record

patch.summary: Update one active document for one active person record

delete.summary: Soft-delete one active document for one active person record

get.summary: Which auth methods this API has enabled. Drives conditional UI on the web/mobile clients.

post.summary: Rotate the refresh token (cookie or body)
Revoke every session of the current user (including this one)

patch.summary: Update the current user's editable profile fields

delete.summary: Hard-delete the current user. Cascades sessions, refresh tokens, OTPs, and OAuth links. Audit events survive.
Unlink an OAuth identity. Refuses if doing so would leave the user with no remaining auth method.

get.description: Includes the current signing key and (during a rotation window) the previous one. Consumers should respect Cache-Control and refresh on `kid` cache miss.
get.summary: Public JWK set for RS256 access-token verification

post.summary: Start or resume passwordless email sign-up/sign-in and return an opaque challenge ID.

401.description: Invalid, expired, used, cross-purpose, or attempt-locked challenge.

post.summary: Email-OTP verify: exchange a valid code for a fresh session (cookies set + TokenPair returned).

post.description: Exchanges a Google ID token (from Google Identity Services on web, or the native Google Sign-In SDK on mobile) for a first-party session. Verification is local: we check the JWT signature, expiry, and audience against the configured `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID_*` client IDs. Google's token is discarded after verification — subsequent requests authenticate with the access/refresh tokens this endpoint returns.

200.description: Provider identity resolved; first-party session issued.

202.description: The provider did not verify the email. A one-time code was sent and must be submitted to /v1/auth/oauth-email/verify.

401.description: The Google identity token failed verification.

post.summary: Sign in with a Google-issued ID token

post.description: Verifies the JWT against Apple's JWKS. On success, resolves or creates the user, issues a session, sets HttpOnly cookies, and returns a TokenPair. See docs/auth/apple-signin.md for the auto-link decision matrix.

200.description: Provider identity resolved; first-party session issued.

202.description: Apple did not verify the email. A one-time code was sent and must be submitted to /v1/auth/oauth-email/verify.

401.description: The token failed verification, or an unlinked Apple identity omitted email entirely.

post.summary: Exchange an Apple identity token for an API session.

401.description: The challenge is missing, used, expired, or locked.

post.summary: Resend an OTP challenge when its server-controlled cooldown has elapsed.

401.description: Invalid or expired code. Missing, used, expired, locked, and wrong-code challenges return the same response.

post.summary: Complete an OAuth sign-in by proving ownership of an email the provider did not mark as verified.

info.title: Turborepo Full Template API
info.description: REST API surface. All operations live under /v1.

updatedAt.description: ISO timestamp of last document update.
ISO timestamp of last person update.

refreshToken.description: Refresh token (mobile clients without a cookie jar pass it in the body). Ignored when an `access_token` cookie is present and valid.

accessToken.description: Signed access JWT. Also set as the `access_token` HttpOnly cookie; mobile clients consume the body field.

refreshToken.description: Signed refresh JWT. Also set as the `refresh_token` HttpOnly cookie.

sessionsRevoked.description: Number of sessions revoked, including the caller's current session.

current.description: True for the session whose refresh token issued the current request.

emailVerified.description: ISO timestamp of email verification, or `null` if unverified.

phoneVerified.description: ISO timestamp of phone verification, or `null` if unverified.

roles.description: Authorization roles. Snapshot also lives in the access-JWT claims; reflects this user's current grants.

email.description: Email address to send the one-time code to. Treated case-insensitively.

challengeId.description: Opaque UUID identifying one OTP challenge.

resendAfterSec.description: Seconds until another delivery may be requested.

challengeId.description: Opaque UUID identifying one OTP challenge.

code.description: The 6-digit one-time code. In non-production `NODE_ENV`, the API accepts the literal `"000000"`.

idToken.description: Google-issued ID token (JWT) from Google Identity Services or the native SDK.
Apple-issued identity token (JWT) from Sign in with Apple JS or the native SDK.

fullName.description: Optional name payload Apple sends only on the very first sign-in. Used as a hint when creating a new User.

challengeId.description: Opaque UUID identifying one OTP challenge.
Opaque UUID identifying one OTP challenge.

code.description: One-time code sent to the email contained in the verified provider token.
